abstract: Synthetic homeopathy fixture for pipeline validation. A materia medica entry and repertory-style rubric describe a symptom totality with chill, thirst, restlessness, and aggravation at night. The constitutional pattern is compared by differential remedy logic; one complementary remedy follows well, while an antidote relationship is noted. This fixture is not a clinical recommendation.
case_reasoning: The remedy was selected because the symptom totality matched restlessness, thirst, and night aggravation.
constitutional_descriptions: The constitutional pattern includes restlessness, anxiety, and marked thirst.
differential_remedy_logic: Differential remedy logic compares similar rubrics before choosing a remedy.
intervention_sequencing: A complementary remedy follows well after the acute remedy in the described sequence.
remedy_relationships: A complementary remedy follows well; an antidote relationship is also noted.
symptom_pattern_relationships: Symptom totality connects chill, thirst, restlessness, and aggravation at night.
text:
Materia medica and repertory notes describe a symptom totality with chill, thirst, restlessness, and aggravation at night.

The constitutional pattern includes restlessness, anxiety, and marked thirst. Differential remedy logic compares similar rubrics before choosing a remedy.

A complementary remedy follows well after the acute remedy in the described sequence, while an antidote relationship is also noted. A case reasoning note reports clinical improvement without making a general treatment claim.
title: Sample homeopathy materia medica and repertory reasoning fixture
